scope: Elastos child coinbase reward address -> pool identity registry for merge-mined child-miner attribution.

source.name: project-maintained elastos child-payout registry
source.notes: Maps a child-chain coinbase payout/reward address to a project-stable pool slug, derived from authenticated AuxPoW linkage: each address consistently co-occurs with one pool's independently-attributed Bitcoin parent coinbase across many merge-mined blocks. Pools that rotate their child payout address per block (notably ViaBTC on Namecoin/Syscoin) cannot be pinned to a single address and are intentionally omitted. Shared/protocol/relay outputs that appear across many pools are excluded.

evidence.btc_parent_pool_reconciliation: 292 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'antpool': 292} (purity 1.000).
evidence.note: reward address is the reliable key; minerinfo 'Antpool' is also stamped by secpool

evidence.btc_parent_pool_reconciliation: 29 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'braiins-pool': 28, 'redrock-pool': 1} (purity 0.966).
177 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'f2pool': 177} (purity 1.000).
4 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'poolin': 4} (purity 1.000).
55 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'secpool': 55} (purity 1.000).
144 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'viabtc': 144} (purity 1.000).
